name: Components of a Vector
description: Breaking a vector into parts—the x-component, y-component, and z-component

content.overview: Instead of thinking of a vector as one arrow, think of it as pieces: how much in the x direction, how much in the y direction. Components make calculations easy. {{viz:diagram-01}}
content.explanation:
Any vector can be broken into components. {{viz:diagram-02}}

**2D Example:**

A vector pointing northeast with magnitude 10 can be split:
- x-component (east): 10 × cos(45°) ≈ 7.07
- y-component (north): 10 × sin(45°) ≈ 7.07

Instead of one arrow pointing northeast, think of it as 7.07 units east PLUS 7.07 units north. {{viz:anim-01}}

**Finding Components:**

If a vector makes angle θ with the x-axis and has magnitude R:
- Vₓ = R × cos(θ)
- Vᵧ = R × sin(θ)
- (Vᵤ = R × cos(φ) if 3D, where φ is different angle)

**Why Components?**

Adding vectors with components is EASY:
- A = (3, 4)
- B = (1, 2)
- A + B = (3+1, 4+2) = (4, 6)

Much simpler than geometry! {{viz:chart-01}}

**Reconstructing from Components:**

Given components (Vₓ, Vᵧ), find magnitude and angle:
- Magnitude: R = √(Vₓ² + Vᵧ²)
- Angle: θ = arctan(Vᵧ/Vₓ)
keyPoints: Break any vector into x, y, (z) components
Vₓ = R cos(θ), Vᵧ = R sin(θ)
Components are perpendicular to each other
content.whyItMatters: Components are the key to all vector calculations in physics. They transform geometry into simple algebra.
